title: The One with the Memorial Service
weakest_section: 17:42-18:04 range with romantic dialogue that's more dramatic than comedic

jokes.text: Oh, great. A faster way to tell people that I'm unemployed and childless.
jokes.setup: Ross excitedly shares the alumni website where people post what they're up to
jokes.punchline: Monica's bitter realization that she has nothing positive to share
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor - Monica's competitive nature makes this self-deprecation especially funny. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: Remember Andrea Rich? - Tall girl, wouldn't sleep with you? - Uh-huh. Well, her Internet company went under, and she lost an ear in a boating accident.
jokes.setup: Ross casually asking if Chandler remembers someone from college
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and darkly comic misfortunes that have befallen her
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates beautifully from professional failure to bizarre accident. The specificity sells it.

jokes.text: Bet she'd sleep with you now.
jokes.setup: Learning about Andrea's terrible misfortunes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's darkly opportunistic observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler-style dark wit. Maximally economical and calls back beautifully to the setup.

jokes.text: No, I already e-mailed her.
jokes.setup: Chandler's suggestion that she might sleep with Ross now
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact admission he already tried
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ross's awkward opportunism delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Could only come from Ross.

jokes.text: Wait a second. You split with Carol because you had different interests? I think you split with Carol because you had one very similar interest.
jokes.setup: Reading Ross's euphemistic description of his divorce from Carol
jokes.punchline: Chandler's clever wordplay about their shared interest in women
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that takes a beat to land. Smart writing that rewards attention and revisiting.

jokes.text: What, did you think you learned how to do it in the last two minutes?
jokes.setup: Ross presumably trying the ball trick again off-screen
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mocking of Ross's persistence despite obvious inability
jokes.explanation: Solid but predictable physical comedy follow-up. Gets the job done but nothing special.

jokes.text: Also, I cloned a dinosaur in my lab. She is now my girlfriend. I don't care what society says, it's the best sex I've ever had.
jokes.setup: The guys deciding to mess with Ross's alumni profile
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd and inappropriate additions to his profile
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation that's appropriately ridiculous. The paleontology connection makes it earned rather than random.

jokes.text: Dude! Uh, I think you made it clear you can't be trusted with the ball inside the house. And send.
jokes.setup: Ross returning and trying to stop them from posting the fake profile
jokes.punchline: Adding insult to injury by posting more embarrassing content
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the prank with nice callback structure. Chandler's mischievous side.

jokes.text: It was fun. Right up until Chandler got a finger in the eye. Oh, no. Who did that? Chandler.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking who hurt Chandler
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Chandler hurt himself
jokes.explanation: Basic misdirection joke with decent structure but predictable punchline. Serviceable.

jokes.text: It's a stuffed animal, you know? It's for kids. Not for adults. I know that.
jokes.setup: Rachel gently suggesting Joey might be too attached to Huggsy
jokes.punchline: Joey's defensive response that actually confirms his attachment
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Joey's childlike nature. His defensive tone sells the joke.

jokes.text: Rachel, let's be clear on this, okay? I do not love Huggsy. I like him a normal amount.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to appear mature about the stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: His overly defensive clarification that undermines his point
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey denial humor. The phrase 'normal amount' about a stuffed penguin is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: All right. Well, Emma loves him. Yeah. Well, why wouldn't she? He's a wonderful person.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Emma's attachment to the toy
jokes.punchline: Joey referring to a stuffed animal as a 'person'
jokes.explanation: Joey's anthropomorphizing of Huggsy reveals his emotional attachment. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I've tried everything to feel better. I even tried writing a song about it... but I can't think of anything that rhymes with: [SCREAMS]
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her attempts to get over Mike
jokes.punchline: Her typically unconventional songwriting approach involving screaming
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe - her unique approach to music and emotional expression. Original and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, good, because I've already thought of three. Four! I just thought of a fourth.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying Monica can make cruel jokes about Mike later
jokes.punchline: Monica's eager admission she's already prepared multiple cruel jokes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness applied to cruel jokes. The real-time addition of a fourth joke sells it.

jokes.text: All right, if that's what you want. That was a test, and you just failed. Damn it! Rookie mistake.
jokes.setup: Phoebe seeming to change her mind about seeing Mike
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was a test and Monica's self-recrimination
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Monica's competitive self-criticism. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: I have sex with dinosaurs?
jokes.setup: Ross discovering what Chandler and Joey posted on his profile
jokes.punchline: His horrified realization of what people will think
jokes.explanation: Solid callback with Ross's characteristic mortification. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Not only is it not funny, it's physically impossible. Okay? Depending on the species, I'd have to have a 6-foot-long-- It's not funny!
jokes.setup: Ross trying to defend himself against the dinosaur sex joke
jokes.punchline: His characteristic over-analysis leading to an even more inappropriate direction
jokes.explanation: Peak Ross behavior - his academic mind making everything worse. The cut-off creates perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: I respectfully disagree.
jokes.setup: Ross saying the dinosaur sex joke isn't funny
jokes.punchline: Chandler's overly formal disagreement
jokes.explanation: Chandler's mock-formal delivery contrasts beautifully with the crude subject matter.

jokes.text: I don't have a page. Oh, I respectfully disagree!
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he doesn't have an alumni page
jokes.punchline: Ross using Chandler's own phrase against him
jokes.explanation: Quick callback showing Ross learning Chandler's tactics. Good escalation of their prank war.

jokes.text: Look at you. All sweet and innocent, sleeping like an angel. With Emma's chubby little hands wrapped around you.
jokes.setup: Joey presumably sneaking into Emma's room
jokes.punchline: His creepy-sweet monologue to a stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's attachment through his tender words. The visual of him talking to a penguin amplifies the humor.

jokes.text: Step away from the crib! I have a weapon! It's okay! It's okay, Rach! It's me! Put down the scrunchie.
jokes.setup: Rachel catching Joey in Emma's room at night
jokes.punchline: Her 'weapon' being a scrunchie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from threatening to absurd. The scrunchie detail is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: This isn't over.
jokes.setup: Joey having to leave Huggsy with Emma
jokes.punchline: His ominous threat to a baby about a stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey petulance delivered with action movie gravity. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Because I told everyone he slept with dinosaurs. Well, that's clearly a joke. This could easily be true.
jokes.setup: Monica reading that Ross posted Chandler is gay
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logic that the gay claim is more believable than dinosaur sex
jokes.explanation: Good logic joke with nice contrast between the two claims. Chandler's self-aware humor.

jokes.text: No, he's not here. Yeah, this is his wife. Yeah. Well, it came as quite a shock to me too. Well, I guess I should've known. Yeah. He just kept making me watch Moulin Rouge.
jokes.setup: Monica answering a congratulatory call about Chandler coming out
jokes.punchline: Her commitment to the bit with specific 'evidence'
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature extends to selling the lie. The Moulin Rouge detail is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm dead?
jokes.setup: Ross discovering Chandler's retaliation
jokes.punchline: The shock of learning about his own death
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective escalation of the prank war. Ross's delivery sells the shock.

jokes.text: Well, how you died was funny. Oh, please. Hit by a blimp?
jokes.setup: Ross complaining that posting his death isn't funny
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific cause of death
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd death. 'Hit by a blimp' is both unexpected and somehow plausible for Ross.

jokes.text: It kills over one Americans every year.
jokes.setup: Ross dismissing death by blimp as implausible
jokes.punchline: Chandler's fake statistic delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake statistic with perfect deadpan delivery. The grammatical awkwardness ('one Americans') makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You wanna talk about people's feelings? You should have heard how hurt Professor Stern was... when I told him I wouldn't be able to go with him to Key West!
jokes.setup: Ross accusing Chandler of messing with people's feelings
jokes.punchline: Ross's mock-hurt about fictional romantic disappointment
jokes.explanation: Ross's indignant commitment to the gay storyline. Good escalation with specific details.

jokes.text: Because I'm on my way to buy some Photoshop software... and a stack of gay porn. That's right. Your coming-out is about to get real graphic!
jokes.setup: Ross threatening retaliation for the death post
jokes.punchline: His elaborate plan for visual revenge
jokes.explanation: Ross's nerdy approach to revenge (Photoshop) combined with his shocked boldness. Character-appropriate escalation.

jokes.text: What are you, animals? It's 4:00 in the afternoon.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what Phoebe and Mike would be doing
jokes.punchline: Her assumption about afternoon sex
jokes.explanation: Monica's uptight sensibilities about appropriate timing. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Phoebe, that's how it starts. 'I don't need to eat the cake, I'll just smell the icing. Why not just a little sliver?' Or, 'Okay, just a slice or two.' You know, the next thing you know, you're 210 pounds... and you get wedged in going down the tunnel slide.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to call Mike
jokes.punchline: Monica's elaborate and specific analogy to food addiction
jokes.explanation: Peak Monica - her food issues projected onto relationship advice. The tunnel slide detail is wonderfully specific and visual.

jokes.text: This is your cell phone? This is your current cell phone?
jokes.setup: Phoebe handing over her phone
jokes.punchline: Monica's shock at Phoebe's ancient phone
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Phoebe's resistance to modern technology. Monica's disbelief sells it.

jokes.text: Yes. It reminds me of a simpler time.
jokes.setup: Monica's incredulity about the old phone
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's earnest justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe romanticism about outdated technology. Her sincerity makes it funny.

jokes.text: Hey! No! - No! - Give it to me! - You can't have it. - Give it to me! - No.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to get Phoebe's purse
jokes.punchline: Their childish physical struggle
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Gets some laughs but nothing special or memorable.

jokes.text: Damn you, Monica Geller hyphen Bing!
jokes.setup: Monica successfully getting the phone from Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's formal curse using Monica's full hyphenated name
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's dramatic formality in defeat. The hyphenated name detail makes it more official and funny.

jokes.text: Hey, look who's here. It's Joey. And he brought home a friend.
jokes.setup: Rachel announcing Joey's return
jokes.punchline: The assumption about what kind of 'friend' Joey brought
jokes.explanation: Sets up the expectation based on Joey's reputation. Serviceable setup for the reveal.

jokes.text: Joey, Emma's right here. You promised not to bring girls home in the middle of the day anymore.
jokes.setup: Rachel seeing Joey with his 'friend'
jokes.punchline: Her assumption and reference to his previous behavior
jokes.explanation: Establishes Joey's reputation and Rachel's parental concerns. Sets up the misdirection.

jokes.text: No, no, no, it's not a girl. It's... a brand-new Huggsy!
jokes.setup: Everyone expecting Joey brought home a woman
jokes.punchline: The reveal it's just another stuffed penguin
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff showing Joey's priorities. His excited delivery sells his attachment.

jokes.text: You know what? When I was a little girl, I had a little pink pony named Cotton. Oh, I loved her so much. I took her everywhere. I would braid her tail--
jokes.setup: Rachel beginning a sentimental story about childhood toys
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off before making her point
jokes.explanation: Setup for Joey's interruption. The story itself isn't the joke - the interruption is.

jokes.text: Make the transfer!
jokes.setup: Rachel's sentimental story about childhood attachment
jokes.punchline: Joey's impatient interruption with business-like language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey impatience with formal 'transaction' language about stuffed animals. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Should I be concerned that a button fell off the old Huggsy and I can't find it?
jokes.setup: The Huggsy transfer discussion
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual mention of a potential choking hazard
jokes.explanation: Joey's oblivious mention of danger to a baby. His casual tone contrasts with the concerning content.

jokes.text: Don't worry about it. I swallowed that years ago.
jokes.setup: The concern about the missing button
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual admission he ate it himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic and his blasé attitude about eating non-food items. Economical and character-defining.

jokes.text: But he's the same.
jokes.setup: Everyone explaining why Emma prefers the old Huggsy
jokes.punchline: Joey's repeated insistence they're identical
jokes.explanation: Joey's desperate denial of what everyone else can see. His repetition shows his emotional investment.

jokes.text: You're not the same.
jokes.setup: Joey's insistence the penguins are identical
jokes.punchline: Rachel's pointed observation about Joey himself
jokes.explanation: Rachel's insight cuts to the emotional truth. Joey's changed relationship with Huggsy mirrors his growth.

jokes.text: You know, it's a lot less surprising to do that... after I've buzzed you into the building.
jokes.setup: Monica presumably doing her 'Aha!' entrance again
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dry observation about Monica's surprise entrance
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's logical observation delivered with perfect timing. Deflates Monica's dramatic moment.

jokes.text: Those are my shoes. Oh. Well, when you get over this breakup, we need to go shopping.
jokes.setup: Monica assuming the men's shoes belong to Mike
jokes.punchline: Monica's backhanded comment about Phoebe's shoe choice
jokes.explanation: Monica's fashion judgment combined with her controlling nature. Shows her friendship through criticism.

jokes.text: What are the chances?
jokes.setup: Mike appearing when Phoebe said she ordered Chinese food
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response to the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Monica's dry sarcasm about the obvious setup. Economical delivery of the obvious truth.

jokes.text: One billion Chinese people, and they send Mike!
jokes.setup: The Chinese food delivery excuse
jokes.punchline: Monica's exaggerated statistical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the sarcasm with absurdist math. Monica's logical mind applied to the ridiculous situation.

jokes.text: There's a speakerphone on the base unit.
jokes.setup: Monica asking how Phoebe called Mike without her phones
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact revelation of her backup plan
jokes.explanation: Shows Phoebe's craftiness beneath her flaky exterior. Monica's oversight revealed.

jokes.text: Base unit. Think, Monica, think!
jokes.setup: Realizing she missed the speakerphone option
jokes.punchline: Monica's angry self-coaching
jokes.explanation: Monica's perfectionist frustration with her own oversight. Her competitive self-talk is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Sorry. I guess I was thrown off by the mention of my name.
jokes.setup: Monica telling him the conversation doesn't concern him
jokes.punchline: Mike's dry response to being excluded from a conversation about himself
jokes.explanation: Mike's polite but pointed sarcasm. Good comeback that highlights the absurdity of Monica's position.

jokes.text: Oh, whoa, whoa, whoa. Make room for your friend.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike sitting close together on the couch
jokes.punchline: Monica physically inserting herself between them
jokes.explanation: Monica's controlling intervention made physical. The awkwardness is the humor.

jokes.text: No, no, no. This is dangerous territory. Keep it clean.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike exchanging compliments
jokes.punchline: Monica's referee-like intervention
jokes.explanation: Monica as relationship referee. Her sports metaphor ('keep it clean') fits her competitive nature.

jokes.text: You know, on the way over here, I saw this drunk guy throw up. And then a pigeon ate it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike having a romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Monica's deliberately gross story to kill the mood
jokes.explanation: Monica's desperate mood-killing tactic. The specificity and grossness are perfectly chosen for maximum romance destruction.

jokes.text: So that's what I'd look like if I worked out. And was being serviced by a policeman.
jokes.setup: Ross seeing Chandler's photoshopped revenge pictures
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact analysis of the image
jokes.explanation: Ross's analytical approach to his own pornographic image is perfectly him. The clinical tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Nobody called, nobody wrote anything, nobody cares that I'm dead.
jokes.setup: Ross discovering no one responded to his fake death
jokes.punchline: His devastated realization of his social insignificance
jokes.explanation: Peak Ross self-pity and social anxiety. His genuine hurt makes it both funny and poignant.

jokes.text: Well, the gay community is a lot more vocal than the dead community.
jokes.setup: Ross comparing response levels to different fake news
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical explanation with absurdist premise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic applied to an absurd situation. Chandler's deadpan delivery of this insightful observation is comedy gold.

jokes.text: I can't believe this. Not even my geology lab partner. And I carried that guy.
jokes.setup: Ross's continued dismay about lack of response
jokes.punchline: His specific complaint about an ungrateful lab partner
jokes.explanation: Ross's academic mindset applied to social betrayal. The 'carried that guy' detail shows his helper nature.

jokes.text: Ross, don't press send. Don't press-- No, too late. Too late. It's sent.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to stop Ross from sending the memorial service invitation
jokes.punchline: Ross's triumphant completion of his plan
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of the prank war. Ross's victory moment but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Sorry, so is that picture of you and the policeman.
jokes.setup: Ross sending the memorial service invitation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's equally petty retaliation
jokes.explanation: Tit-for-tat escalation. Economical but predictable revenge move.

jokes.text: It's Monica, okay? That's not Monica! All right, fine! It's original Huggsy!
jokes.setup: Rachel confronting Joey about the lump under his covers
jokes.punchline: Joey's desperate lies followed by confession
jokes.explanation: Joey's childish lying and quick confession. His attachment to the penguin drives the desperation.

jokes.text: I know Emma wants him, but he's mine and I need him. She's being unreasonable.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he won't give up Huggsy
jokes.punchline: Calling a baby unreasonable for wanting a toy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - competing with a baby and finding the baby unreasonable. His childlike reasoning is spot-on.

jokes.text: It's because it reminds her of her Uncle Joey.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Emma loves the penguin
jokes.punchline: The sweet revelation about Joey's resemblance to Huggsy
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that explains the emotional connection. Earned sentiment rather than cheap manipulation.

jokes.text: If Huggsy means that much to Emma, then... Well, she can have him.
jokes.setup: Joey realizing Emma's attachment
jokes.punchline: His reluctant but loving sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Joey's character growth moment. The comedy comes from his internal struggle rather than the words.

jokes.text: No, forget it! Forget it! I can't do it!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to be generous
jokes.punchline: His immediate retreat from selflessness
jokes.explanation: Joey's honest self-assessment. His emotional attachment wins over his better angels.

jokes.text: Are you--? You're gonna take Huggsy away from a little child? How do you think I got him in the first place?
jokes.setup: Rachel's moral outrage at taking a toy from a baby
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual admission of his original theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark revelation about Joey's origin story with Huggsy. His matter-of-fact delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: All right, you two. I'm gonna go to the bathroom. I don't want anything going on while I'm gone. Here's a few things you can discuss: Mucus, fungus, and the idea of me and Ross doing it.
jokes.setup: Monica leaving Phoebe and Mike alone briefly
jokes.punchline: Her list of romance-killing conversation topics
jokes.explanation: Monica's controlling nature extended to conversation management. The escalation from medical terms to personal grossness is perfect.

jokes.text: Just to be with you one more night. I want that too. But is that gonna make it too hard? It can't be any harder than this.
jokes.setup: Mike expressing his desire to spend one more night together
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logical but heartbreaking response
jokes.explanation: More emotional than comedic, but the situation itself has inherent comedy from Monica's failed interference.

jokes.text: If I had known the last time I saw you would be the last time, I-- I would have stopped to memorize your face, the way you moved. Everything about you. If I had known the last time I kissed you would be the last time... I never would have stopped.
jokes.setup: Mike's romantic speech about missed last moments
jokes.punchline: Sets up Monica's reaction
jokes.explanation: Romantic setup for Monica's intervention. More functional than funny on its own.

jokes.text: Kiss him, you fool! What? Didn't you hear that speech? If you don't kiss him, then I will.
jokes.setup: Mike's romantic speech
jokes.punchline: Monica being moved despite herself and threatening to kiss him
jokes.explanation: Monica's romantic side overruling her controlling mission. Her threat to kiss Mike herself is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I knew you'd be here! Aw, crap.
jokes.setup: Manny arriving to interrupt the romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Mike's frustrated recognition of the parallel situation
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure showing both sides have the same support system problem.

jokes.text: Well, you're not doing a very good job. Excuse me? What's with the kissing? Hey, at least I knew where my guy was.
jokes.setup: The two relationship guardians meeting
jokes.punchline: Their competitive argument about effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation between the two interferers. Shows Monica's competitive nature even in this context.

jokes.text: You didn't hear the speech. I've heard the speech. If he knew it was gonna be the last time-- Hey, it was very moving. You're just heartless! You're weak! You're weird! Your pants are undone. Oh!
jokes.setup: Monica and Manny arguing about their methods
jokes.punchline: The escalating insults ending with a wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Standard escalating argument with physical comedy button. Gets the job done but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Where'd they go? Damn it. Well, we blew it. I blame myself. And I blame you too.
jokes.setup: Realizing Phoebe and Mike escaped during their fight
jokes.punchline: Monica's fair distribution of blame
jokes.explanation: Monica's logical approach to failure distribution. Shows her fairness even in defeat.

jokes.text: It's been an hour, and not one of my classmates has shown up? I tell you, when I actually die, some people are gonna get seriously haunted.
jokes.setup: Ross's disappointment at the memorial turnout
jokes.punchline: His threat of supernatural revenge
jokes.explanation: Ross's petty nature extended to the afterlife. The specific threat of haunting is perfectly Ross.

jokes.text: Hi. Glad you could come. Please come in. Hi. You're Chandler Bing, right?
jokes.setup: Someone finally arriving at the memorial
jokes.punchline: Sets up the revelation about why he came
jokes.explanation: Setup for the bigger jokes to come. Functional but not funny on its own.

jokes.text: Oh, yes. Yes, let me take your coat. Thanks. Uh, I'm so sorry about Ross, it's... No, at least he died doing what he loved. Watching blimps.
jokes.setup: Tom expressing condolences
jokes.punchline: Chandler's commitment to the absurd death story
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Chandler's deadpan commitment to the bit. 'Watching blimps' as a hobby is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Actually, I barely knew him. I came because I heard Chandler's news. Do you know if he's seeing anyone?
jokes.setup: Tom seeming to mourn Ross
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's actually interested in Chandler romantically
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing the gay post is attracting attention. Good structure building to the reveal.

jokes.text: Yes, he is. Me. What? You mean--? Oh.
jokes.setup: Tom asking if Chandler is available
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward claim to be his own boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Chandler's quick thinking leads to an absurd solution. The awkwardness drives the humor.

jokes.text: Can I ask you a personal question? How do you shave your beard so close?
jokes.setup: Tom accepting Chandler's relationship status
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific question about grooming
jokes.explanation: Odd specific question that adds to the awkwardness. Shows Tom's continued interest despite the relationship claim.

jokes.text: I'm dead and no one cares? I look like a man?
jokes.setup: Both discovering the unexpected results of their pranks
jokes.punchline: Their parallel complaints about different problems
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure showing both their pranks backfired in unexpected ways. Economical and well-matched.

jokes.text: And Monica, it's probably the way you stand!
jokes.setup: Discussing Chandler's masculine appearance problem
jokes.punchline: Ross's helpful suggestion about Monica's posture
jokes.explanation: Ross's misguided helpfulness. His academic mind trying to solve the wrong problem.

jokes.text: I mean, I went to that school for four years and I didn't have an impact on anyone?
jokes.setup: Ross's continued existential crisis about his significance
jokes.punchline: His genuine hurt about making no impression
jokes.explanation: Ross's genuine insecurity coming through. More poignant than funny but sets up Chandler's response.

jokes.text: Well, that's not true. You had an impact on me. I mean, it's 15 years later and we're still best friends. Doesn't that count for something?
jokes.setup: Ross's self-pity about having no impact
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sincere but obvious point about their friendship
jokes.explanation: Sweet friendship moment. The humor comes from Chandler's matter-of-fact delivery of obvious affection.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm here for Ross Geller's memorial service.
jokes.setup: Another person arriving at the memorial
jokes.punchline: Sets up Ross's excitement about being remembered
jokes.explanation: Setup line for the bigger payoffs to come. Not funny on its own.

jokes.text: Chandler. Chandler Bing. And I'm not gay. I'm not gay at all.
jokes.setup: Meeting Kori at Ross's memorial
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate and emphatic denial of homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Chandler's desperation to correct the record. His emphasis ('at all') shows his panic.

jokes.text: You are married, though. Don't listen to him. He's in a really bad mood.
jokes.setup: Kori noting Chandler's marital status
jokes.punchline: Dead Ross trying to help his friend's romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Ross's helpful interference from beyond the 'grave.' His excuse for Chandler's behavior is perfectly Ross.

jokes.text: I can't believe that Ross is gone. It is just so sad.
jokes.setup: Kori expressing grief for Ross
jokes.punchline: The irony of mourning someone who's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dramatic irony for Ross's revelation. More setup than punchline.

jokes.text: And he talked so passionately about science. I always remembered him.
jokes.setup: Kori sharing memories of Ross
jokes.punchline: Her genuine fond memory of his academic passion
jokes.explanation: Sets up Ross's validation. Her memory of his passion is touching and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And if heaven has a door... I'm sure he's pressing his ear up against it and listening intently.
jokes.setup: Kori's touching tribute to Ross
jokes.punchline: Chandler's joke about Ross eavesdropping from the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Ross literally listening nearby. Chandler's wit applied to the absurd situation.

jokes.text: I thought so many times about calling him and asking him out. I guess I really missed my chance.
jokes.setup: Kori expressing regret about not pursuing Ross
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's about to reveal himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Ross's dramatic entrance. Creates maximum dramatic irony.

jokes.text: No, you didn't!
jokes.setup: Kori saying she missed her chance with Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's dramatic resurrection reveal
jokes.explanation: Ross's dramatic timing for maximum effect. His desperation for romantic validation drives the reveal.

jokes.text: I'm still alive! Kori, I know this is a big surprise for you. It's a long story. But the things you just said really made my day.
jokes.setup: Ross revealing he's alive to the shocked woman
jokes.punchline: His casual explanation of the elaborate deception
jokes.explanation: Ross's typically awkward handling of a dramatic moment. His priorities (validation over explanation) are perfectly him.

jokes.text: The fact that you're here means more to me... than if this room were filled with people.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to explain the situation to Kori
jokes.punchline: His sincere but awkward attempt at romance after faking his death
jokes.explanation: Ross trying to be romantic while explaining his death hoax. His sincerity in an absurd situation is very Ross.

jokes.text: You sick freak! Who does that? I can't believe I had a crush on you.
jokes.setup: Ross's romantic overture after the death reveal
jokes.punchline: Kori's horrified reaction and immediate revulsion
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from romantic interest to complete revulsion. Her reaction is the reasonable response to Ross's insanity.

jokes.text: Did you hear that? Kori Weston had a crush on me!
jokes.setup: Kori's angry departure after calling him a sick freak
jokes.punchline: Ross focusing only on the crush admission, ignoring the revulsion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross selective hearing - taking only the validation while ignoring the condemnation. Peak Ross behavior and brilliant character writing.

jokes.text: No, Mom, I am not dead.
jokes.setup: The aftermath of the memorial service prank
jokes.punchline: Ross having to reassure his mother about his mortality
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the real-world consequences of the prank. Ross dealing with parental concern.

jokes.text: It was just a practical joke between Chandler and me, but it's over, okay?
jokes.setup: Explaining the death hoax to his mother
jokes.punchline: Calling the elaborate scheme a simple 'practical joke'
jokes.explanation: Ross's typical minimization of his dramatic behavior. 'Practical joke' understates the memorial service absurdity.

jokes.text: Actually, no. Even if I had died, you would not be left childless. Monica?
jokes.setup: Presumably his mother expressing fear of being childless
jokes.punchline: Ross's reminder that Monica exists too
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic with Ross making sure Monica gets acknowledged. Shows family hierarchy concerns even in death scenarios.

tmdb_overview: Chandler's jokey entry on his and Ross's college website leads to a faux burial service for the allegedly dead and obviously unpopular Gellar. Meanwhile, Joey is unwilling to give his favorite stuffed animal, Hugsy, to Emma.
